nodes.label: AI as summarization and primer tool for complex regulatory documents
nodes.label_negative: Manual reading and synthesis without AI assistance
nodes.grounding_span: I often use AI to summarize new regulatory documents (orders, testimony, exhibits, etc) or to create quick primers on regulatory processes to date.

nodes.label: AI as collaborative discovery partner
nodes.label_negative: AI as a one-shot answer provider
nodes.grounding_span: As I identify those areas with AI, our conversation expands.

nodes.label: AI as efficiency booster under tight deadlines
nodes.grounding_span: I was able to produce a report from 20-30 pages of meeting notes in just 2 hours.

nodes.label: Human control over high-stakes legal writing
nodes.label_negative: AI autonomy in legal document production
nodes.grounding_span: I often like to handle those myself. I would not want a minor style or formatting error to delay a time sensitive filing.

nodes.label: AI as a tool not a threat to job security
nodes.label_negative: AI as a job replacement threat
nodes.grounding_span: AI is more of a tool in the toolbox than something I fear will take over my job.

nodes.label: Productivity and time efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: I was able to produce a report from 20-30 pages of meeting notes in just 2 hours.

nodes.label: Accuracy and compliance in legal work
nodes.grounding_span: I would not want a minor style or formatting error to delay a time sensitive filing.

nodes.label: Epistemic understanding and learning
nodes.grounding_span: Often I am reading the documents for the first time, and may not know all of the things that I need to follow-up on.

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for time-sensitive synthesis
nodes.grounding_span: I felt particularly satisfied when I was able to produce a report from 20-30 pages of meeting notes in just 2 hours.

nodes.label: Preference for human control over legal writing
nodes.grounding_span: I often like to handle those myself. I would not want a minor style or formatting error to delay a time sensitive filing.

nodes.label: Optimistic openness to expanding AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I could see it expanding. If the models become more proficient at extracting and synthesizing regulatory information they could write more final products.

nodes.label: Iterative validation and follow-up questioning
nodes.grounding_span: I will then read through what the AI gave me and then go back and skim through the document. If it seems like the AI skipped major chapters or topics I will focus on those with the AI and ask more specific questions.

nodes.label: Domain-specific boundary setting for AI use
nodes.grounding_span: There are some tasks that will be reviewed by our legal department and have specific style and formatting requirements. I often like to handle those myself.
